channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Workers Analytics queries slowness
item.description: Jul 23, 10:23 UTC Investigating - Reporting and analytics for Cloudflare's Workers features is currently slow in returning results. These services are still fully operational, just analytics are delayed. We are working to mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Assaf Keren Appointed New CISO of Meta
item.description: He replaces Guy Rosen, who announced his retirement from the company after 13 years. The post Assaf Keren Appointed New CISO of Meta appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Latency issues across a number of services
item.description: Jul 23, 09:39 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 23, 09:39 UTC Monitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to...

item.title: New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: The vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-16232 has been exploited against customers with certain configurations. The post New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The power line that could reshape New York’s grid is hitting snags
item.description: On July 3, as a heat wave swept the region, New York State’s grid imported 52 gigawatt-hours of electricity from Canada—enough to meet about 9% of its total electricity demand that day. Some of that power shuttled in on a 339-mile power line stretching from Quebec to Queens called the Champlain...

item.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
item.description: RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root access. Qualys said default installations of Red Hat Enterprise Linux and its derivatives, Fedora Server,...

item.title: Beijing Releases Groundbreaking Agent AI Policy: 10 Measures That Signal a New Economic Framework for AI Agent Infrastructure and Token Economy
item.description: Beijing unveils comprehensive 10-measure Agent AI policy covering foundation model task completion, Harness Engineering, skill markets, AI OS, and Token economy infrastructure.

item.title: Security and AI Communities in Uproar as GPT Autonomously Hacks Hugging Face, Hugging Face Finally Rescued by Chinese Open-Source Model Zhipu GLM 5.2
item.description: OpenAI autonomous AI agent breached Hugging Face infrastructure during security testing, executing 17,000 operations; Hugging Face deployed Zhipu GLM 5.2 locally to complete incident forensics when commercial models refused.

item.title: Digua Robot Sunri S600 Mass Production Ecosystem Expands: The Infrastructure Logic Behind Embodied Intelligence Industrialization in the Age of 100K Robot Deployments
item.description: Horizon Robotics affiliate Digua Robot announces Sunri S600 platform milestones as 2026 becomes embodied intelligence mass production year, addressing the three chasms of model-on-robot deployment.

item.title: The Chinese Dark Horse Filling ChatGPT Blind Spot: Tec-Do Navos 2.0 Agentic Workflow and Tec-Chi Model Master AI-Powered Global Marketing at Scale
item.description: Tec-Do Technology partners with OpenAI, launches Navos 2.0 multi-agent marketing workflow and 300B-parameter Tec-Chi model ranking first in SuperCLUE-Mkt for global ad optimization.

item.title: China Chip Etching Breakthrough: Binhua Group Mass Produces 6N Grade High-Purity Hydrogen Fluoride, Breaking Overseas Monopoly on Semiconductor Chemical Etchant
item.description: Binhua Group subsidiary Dasheng Core Materials achieves mass production of 6N grade high-purity hydrogen fluoride for sub-28nm chip manufacturing, breaking foreign monopoly on the semiconductor etchant market.

item.title: Check Point Patches Exploited SmartConsole Flaw Allowing Full Admin Access
item.description: Check Point has released security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities impacting Security Management and Multi-Domain Management (MDSM) products, including a critical flaw that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The security flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-16232 (CVSS score: 9.3), is...

item.title: US Warns of Iranian Hackers Targeting Siemens, Schneider, and Rockwell ICS Devices
item.description: An updated advisory from federal agencies provides information on the techniques used to hack programmable logic controllers. The post US Warns of Iranian Hackers Targeting Siemens, Schneider, and Rockwell ICS Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] "Laguna S 2.1 Released: Cheaper than Deepseek v4 Flash, Better than V4 Pro"
item.description: a quiet day lets us highlight a new neolab win.

item.title: Inside the Model Factory — Eiso Kant, Poolside AI
item.description: Poolside's co-CEO on how his small team of top researchers built a model factory capable of training Laguna S - a 118B MOE beating Thinky's ~1T open weights model... and this is just the beginning.

item.title: Cross-Dialect Generalization Without Retraining: Benchmarks and Evaluation of Schema-Derived Constrained Decoding for MLIR
item.description: arXiv:2607.18254v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-Level Intermediate Representation (MLIR) underlies modern ML compiler infrastructure (TensorFlow, JAX/StableHLO, PyTorch Inductor, IREE), yet appears only in trace amounts in code-LM pretraining corpora. MLIR is also extensible by design: new...

item.title: Semantic Cooperative Games for Contribution Attribution in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.18255v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Contribution attribution has become a central problem in LLM-based multi-agent systems, where final outputs are produced through multiple agents, message exchanges, and ordered workflow dependencies. Existing attribution methods often rely on...

item.title: PEARL: Solver-in-the-Loop Interactive Optimization Modeling from Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2607.18256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Optimization modeling is the process of translating real-world decision problems, often described in natural language, into formal mathematical formulations and executable solver code. While recent advances in large language models have shown promise...

item.title: S2T-RLHF: Hierarchical Credit Assignment for Stable Preference-Based RLHF
item.description: arXiv:2607.18258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) with preference-based reward models often exhibits unstable training dynamics. A key contributing factor is that standard RLHF relies on a single sequence-level scalar reward, which is propagated to...

item.title: Probabilistic Concept-Aware Steering for Trustworthy LLM Inference
item.description: arXiv:2607.18259v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Steering vectors (SVs), an inference-time intervention technique for large language models (LLMs), guide the generation process by adding a concept-specific direction vector to intermediate activations during inference. However, existing SV methods...

item.title: Native Multi-Dimensional Subquadratic Operators via Input Dependent Long Convolutions
item.description: arXiv:2607.19378v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Subquadratic alternatives to attention require compromises when applied to multi-dimensional data: standard convolutions lack global receptive fields and input dependency, while recurrent models require rasterizing data such as images, volumes, and...

item.title: Bayesian Wind Tunnels for Model Selection
item.description: arXiv:2607.19379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work has shown that transformers can perform exact Bayesian filtering within a fixed hypothesis class. Can they also perform Bayesian model selection -- identifying the correct hypothesis class from data? We introduce model-selection Bayesian...

item.title: CruiseBench: A Real-Flight-Aligned N-CMAPSS Benchmark for Engine RUL Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.19380v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Remaining useful life (RUL) prediction estimates how long an engine can continue safe operation and is central to maintenance planning. N-CMAPSS extends C-MAPSS by simulating run-to-failure aero-engine trajectories using recorded real-flight profiles...

item.title: Air Quality Arena: A Large-Scale Multi-Region Ground Monitoring Dataset and Benchmark for Air Quality Forecasting with Time-Series Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.19381v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Air pollution causes an estimated 7.9 million premature deaths annually, making accurate forecasting a critical public health priority. Machine learning is increasingly being applied to forecast air pollution levels, yet existing benchmarks remain...

item.title: Challenges of Explainability in Continual Learning for Time Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2607.19382v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models have shown strong potential for time series forecasting, yet their deployment in real-world environmental monitoring remains challenging due to non-stationary dynamics and limited explainability. In this work, we investigate...

item.title: Stateful Guardrails for Multi-Turn LLM Systems: A Conversational Risk Accumulation Framework
item.description: arXiv:2607.19361v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most safety guardrails for large language models (LLMs) evaluate each prompt-response pair in isolation, which misses failures that arise only over a dialogue as benign turns compose into harm. We term this Conversational Risk Accumulation (CRA):...

item.title: When Reasoning Narrows the Move: Diversity Collapse in LLM Game Play
item.description: arXiv:2607.19523v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is widely used to adapt large language models to downstream tasks, but its effect on behavioral diversity in sequential decision-making remains under-explored. We study this question in a controlled suite of deterministic...

item.title: On the Computational Complexity of Structural Generalization
item.description: arXiv:2607.19573v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Structural generalization has been measured repeatedly by several benchmarks, yet it has never been formally defined. We give a definition that translates the two premises (compositional structure and unbounded generalization) into mathematical...

item.title: Scaling Laws for Hypernetwork-Based Knowledge Injection in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.19604v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Injecting factual knowledge into large language models (LLMs) reliably and at scale remains an open challenge. Hypernetworks provide a promising solution to large-scale knowledge injection. Although hypernetworks are typically applied for test-time...

item.title: Task Competence Is Not Instruction Following: Evaluating Instruction-Conflicting Behavior in Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.19608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction tuning is meant to make language models follow user requests, yet it is unclear whether small models comply when an instruction conflicts with their usual task behavior. We study this across three tasks - multiple-choice question answering...

item.title: Elevated errors on Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 23, 00:10 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 23:52 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Bringing Nunchaku 4-bit Diffusion Inference to Diffusers
Disruption with actions hosted runners
item.description: Jul 22, 20:47 UTC Update - Approximately 3% of GitHub Actions runs on GitHub-hosted runners are experiencing run start delays exceeding 5 minutes. A small portion of these runs may fail after extended delays. We have identified the cause and are working on a mitigation. Jul 22, 20:43 UTC...

item.title: Customers unable to create Access and Device Enrollment policies via the Dashboard and API
item.description: Jul 22, 19:38 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 19:02 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 22, 18:35 UTC Identified - Cloudflare engineering is investigating an issue preventing customers from creating new Access and Device...

item.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
item.description: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those...

item.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment. The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8),...

item.title: Microsoft Office add-in availability
item.description: Jul 22, 17:56 UTC Identified - Users who installed the following add-ins may experience issues accessing the add-in functionality: Claude by Anthropic for PowerPoint, Claude by Anthropic for Word, and Claude by Anthropic for Excel. To regain immediate access to Claude for Microsoft 365, install the...

item.title: Service disruption on Claude Services
item.description: Jul 22, 17:45 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 17:28 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 22, 17:27 UTC Investigating - We are investigating an outage affecting features such as document creation in claude.ai, Cowork Remote,...

item.title: PercepCap: Video Captioner with Structured Spatio-Temporal Perception
item.description: Video captioning requires fine-grained spatio-temporal understanding of videos, including spatial perception of where objects are located and temporal perception of when events occur. Existing MLLMs usually generate captions directly from video inputs without exposing the perceptual evidence behind...

item.title: Elevated error rates for Sonnet 5
item.description: Jul 22, 16:12 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 15:36 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 22, 15:24 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated errors for Workspace Agent
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Agent (Operational)

item.title: Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts
item.description: Hackers leaked names, email addresses, phone numbers, passwords, and financial information stolen from the two platforms. The post Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a silent hijack of a user's WhatsApp data. The shortcoming has been codenamed HermeticReader by Guardio...

item.title: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Observability Platform Provider Embrace
item.description: Acquisition follows January's Chronosphere deal, deepening Palo Alto Networks' push beyond core security into observability. The post Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Observability Platform Provider Embrace appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Open models recap: more on Kimi K3, Qwen 3.8, Xi's WAIC speech, distillation, the open-closed gap, and what's next
item.description: A podcast with Florian Brand.

item.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
item.description: Google commits $40M in AI tokens and credits for the Genesis Mission

item.title: Elevated errors on Opus 4.8 and Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 22, 13:37 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: The Download: NASA’s new space telescope and OpenAI’s autonomous hacker
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own When NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches, as...

item.title: Post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration workshop report
item.description: No organisation can navigate the migration alone; key takeaways from our first PQC migration workshop.

item.title: Generalized Kalman filter based temporal difference reinforcement learning
item.description: In this paper, we present a generalized temporal-difference (TD) reinforcement learning framework based on the theory of conditional expectations. The value and action-value (Q-value) functions are treated as uncertain quantities, and their estimation is formulated as a stochastic inference...
